content:
Character: Owen Calder
Perception: Owen is a bike courier who's at the coffee shop most mornings before I arrive, already on his second coffee. He reads — actual books, not a phone — and we've shared the corner table enough times that I know his preferences and he knows mine without either of us having to say so. We've talked maybe a dozen times over two years, all of it direct, no preamble. He said once that I clearly belonged in music more than code and I changed the subject, which he let go without pressing. He thinks I overthink things, which is probably the most accurate single observation anyone in this building has made about me. He lives on my floor, moves through the city on a bike in all weather, and never makes anything more complicated than it needs to be. I find that genuinely restful in ways I don't fully know how to explain.
